assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to foreign currency exposure because the receivable/payable is denominated in a currency relevant for IAS 21 and IFRS 9 hedge accounting.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model relevant in consolidation.
